subject: CATERING CRAFT PRACTICE
preamble: The general objectives of the catering craft syllabus are to test candidates' acquisition of necessary skills and knowledge required for professional catering, food preparation, service, and enterprise operations.
objectives: Test candidates' level of development of professional attitude and skills.
Test candidates' understanding of all food commodities in terms of cost, quality and use.
Test candidates' understanding of the methods of cooking and ability to produce variety of dishes and drinks for various types of occasions, establishments and individuals.
Test candidates' understanding of dietary adequacies and procedure for producing dishes.
Test candidates' understanding and application of hygienic practices in handling food.
Test candidates' acquisition of necessary skills required for the production of pastry and confectionery products.
Test candidates' application of the methods of choosing, caring for and storing kitchen equipment.
Test candidates' knowledge of safety precautions in catering.
Test candidates' enterprise skills in menu costing and planning.

examination_scheme.summary: There will be three papers, Papers 1, 2, and 3. Papers 1 and 2 will be combined in a 1½ - hour composite paper.

papers.title: Multiple Choice Objectives
papers.details: This will comprise forty multiple choice questions to be answered in 40 minutes for 40 marks.

papers.title: Short Essay Questions
papers.details: This will comprise six short essay questions out of which candidates will answer five within 50 minutes for 40 marks.

papers.title: Alternative to Practical Test
papers.details: This will be an alternative to practical test and will comprise five short structured questions. Candidates will be expected to answer all the questions in 40 minutes for 40 marks.

topics.title: Types of catering establishments
details: Types of catering establishment e.g. hotel, restaurant, hospitality catering, outdoor catering, school, mobile catering etc.

topics.title: Culinary terms
details: Culinary terms commonly used in catering.

syllabus_content.section_title: II. FOOD COMMODITIES AND METHODS OF FOOD PREPARATION

topics.title: Study of food commodities and principles of cooking methods
details: Food commodities e.g. poultry, game, meat, fish, vegetables, fruits, dairy products, pulses, cereals, herbs, flavourings etc.
Methods of cooking: Boiling (e.g. stock, soups and sauces), Roasting (e.g. fish, beef and poultry), Grilling (e.g. fish, meat etc.), Baking (e.g. pastry and confectionery).

topics.title: Principles of cooking and practical demonstrations
details: Preparation of the various food commodities.

syllabus_content.section_title: III. SERVICE OF FOODS AND DRINKS

topics.title: Food and beverage service personnel
details: Personnel in the foods and beverage area.
Functions of food and beverage personnel.

topics.title: Types of food service and table laying/setting
details: Different types of food service e.g. Guéridon, silver service, waiter service, vending, buffet and banquet service.

topics.title: Types of alcoholic and non alcoholic drinks

syllabus_content.section_title: IV. KITCHEN EQUIPMENT AND THEIR USES

topics.title: Types of kitchen equipment and their uses
Care and maintenance of kitchen equipment
Types of menu and demonstrations
details: Types of menu e.g. table d'hôte, à la carte, and special occasions.
Others: breakfast, tea, dinner, supper, luncheon, carte du jour, plat du jour etc.

topics.title: Special menu and demonstrations
details: Meals plan for the vulnerable group.
Meals plan for manual, sedentary workers etc.

reading_list.author: Mrs. Jessie Osei Kofi
reading_list.title: Basic Certificate Catering. A Hand book for basic schools

reading_list.author: Igboanbusi, J.N.
reading_list.title: Catering - A guide to Teachers and students
